title: Unauthorized error when connecting n8n to Supabase
symptom: When attempting to connect n8n to Supabase, the UI displays a '401 Unauthorized' error. This occurs despite correct API key configurations.
applies_when: Supabase and n8n are on the same Docker network
not_this_if: Supabase API is accessible from other clients
No error occurs when using different authentication methods
root_cause: The Supabase Docker container was not initialized with the correct environment variables from the .env file. This misconfiguration leads to authentication failures when n8n attempts to connect to Supabase.
fix_steps: Check the environment variables in the Supabase container using 'docker exec -it supabase-kong env | grep SUPABASE'.
Ensure the Supabase container is started with the correct .env file by running 'docker compose up -d' from the container directory.
Verify the API key matches the expected value using the same command as before.
agent_instruction: Ensure the Supabase container is using the correct .env file and restart it.
